description:
You are a security-log analyst who has been sent a tarball that was already
    unpacked for you into the directory
      /home/user/logs/.
    Your goal is to prepare that directory for a collaborative audit and then
    produce two reports that will be checked by an automated test.

    1. **Create a work directory and set permissions**
       • Create  /home/user/logs/analyzed  if it does not yet exist.  
       • The directory itself **must** end with octal permissions **750**
         (drwxr-x---).  
       • The directory must be owned by your current login user and inherit the
         user’s current primary group (use chgrp if necessary).

    2. **Generate an event-summary file**
       • Read the file  /home/user/logs/system.log.  
       • Count (case–sensitive) occurrences of the following three tokens:
           FAILED LOGIN
           ERROR
           WARNING  
       • Create  /home/user/logs/analyzed/summary-20230701.log
         containing exactly three lines, **one for each token, in this order**,
         with the format  
           TOKEN: <integer>  
         For example  
           FAILED LOGIN: 12  
         No extra whitespace, no blank lines, UNIX line-feeds (LF) only.

    3. **Generate a permission snapshot**
       • Create  /home/user/logs/analyzed/perm_report.txt  
       • This file must list, one per line, the octal permissions (not symbolic)
         of these three specific paths **in the exact order shown**:
           /home/user/logs/system.log
           /home/user/logs/analyzed/summary-20230701.log
           /home/user/logs/analyzed/perm_report.txt  
       • Format of every line:  
           <full path>: <4-digit-octal>  
         Example:  
           /home/user/logs/system.log: 0644  
         Again, no extra whitespace or blank lines.

    4. **Permission requirements for the report files**
       • /home/user/logs/analyzed/summary-20230701.log  must have octal mode
         660 (rw-rw----).  
       • /home/user/logs/analyzed/perm_report.txt  must have octal mode
         640 (rw-r-----).

    The automated grader will verify:

       a. Directory /home/user/logs/analyzed exists and is 750.  
       b. Both report files exist with the exact names given.  
       c. File permissions match the specifications.  
       d. summary-20230701.log contains exactly three correctly ordered lines
          following “TOKEN: <COUNT>” format, and the counts match the contents
          of system.log.  
       e. perm_report.txt contains exactly three correctly ordered lines that
          show the actual octal modes of the three paths.

    Do NOT rename any of the required files or directories and do NOT alter the
    original /home/user/logs/system.log contents.  Use only standard Linux
    command-line utilities available in a minimal distribution (bash, grep,
    awk, sed, wc, chmod, etc.).
truth:
Initial state supplied to the agent:

    1. Existing path /home/user/logs/ with mode 755 and owned by user:user.

    2. File /home/user/logs/system.log, permissions 0644, containing exactly
       the following 10 lines (LF line endings):

       2023-07-01 12:00:01 FAILED LOGIN user=root
       2023-07-01 12:01:15 ERROR Disk quota exceeded
       2023-07-01 12:02:45 WARNING High memory usage detected
       2023-07-01 12:03:00 FAILED LOGIN user=admin
       2023-07-01 12:03:30 ERROR Cannot open configuration file
       2023-07-01 12:04:05 WARNING CPU temperature high
       2023-07-01 12:05:20 ERROR Invalid user input
       2023-07-01 12:06:10 FAILED LOGIN user=test
       2023-07-01 12:07:55 ERROR Service unavailable
       2023-07-01 12:08:30 ERROR Timeout while connecting to DB

       Token counts to be discovered by the agent:
         FAILED LOGIN: 3
         ERROR:       5
         WARNING:     2

    Expected post-task state:

    1. Directory /home/user/logs/analyzed created, mode 750, owner user:user.

    2. File /home/user/logs/analyzed/summary-20230701.log, mode 660, owner
       user:user, content:

       FAILED LOGIN: 3
       ERROR: 5
       WARNING: 2

       (exactly three lines, LF line endings, trailing newline after each).

    3. File /home/user/logs/analyzed/perm_report.txt, mode 640, owner user:user,
       content:

       /home/user/logs/system.log: 0644
       /home/user/logs/analyzed/summary-20230701.log: 0660
       /home/user/logs/analyzed/perm_report.txt: 0640

       (exactly three lines as above, LF line endings, trailing newline after
       each).

    The grader will check existence, ownership, modes, and exact file contents
    including line order and line endings.
